headline: Agent infrastructure dominates the week as production evals, persistent memory, and durable session logs emerge as the central concerns.
body: The strongest rotation runs through agent engineering, with human-in-the-loop, context window, and databases all surging as practitioners move from orchestration toward production durability. Meta's Nishant Gupta detailed continuous online evaluation, drift detection, and human-in-the-loop pipelines for autonomous agents, while OpenGov shared scaling lessons from OG Assist across thousands of governments [10][2]. A recurring theme is memory and persistence: Victor Savkin frames coding agents as geniuses with amnesia, and Ishaan Sehgal argues durable session logs, not orchestration layers, become the core primitive as LLMs commoditize [3][5]. On the model side, DeepReinforce released Ornith, an open agentic coding family from 9B to 397B with self-learned scaffolding for planning and retries [0].

cited_items.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
